output.final_deliverable: Data not yet produced because the merged results provided do not include the exact 2024 repository counts or the 2023 baselines by language, nor the primary-use-case mappings. To fulfill the mandate, I will fetch the authoritative figures from GitHub’s Octoverse pages for 2024 (top languages by repository count with 2023 baselines) and 2023 for cross-year comparison, then compute: (a) for each of the top 5 languages in 2024 — repo_count_2024, repo_count_2023, percent_change_2024_vs_2023; (b) identify the leader in 2024 and the language with the largest percentage growth; (c) assemble primary use cases from credible sources (official docs, credible analyses); and (d) provide a trend analysis and two-year overtaking projection under conservative, moderate, and aggressive scenarios. Finally, I will cite all data sources with inline URLs. (Planned data sources: GitHub Octoverse 2024 language distribution page and GitHub Octoverse 2023 language distribution page, plus credible sources for language use cases.)
output.action_summary: The current merged results lack the specific numeric counts needed to answer: (1) the exact top-5 languages by repository count in 2024, (2) the 2023 baseline counts for those languages, (3) the year-over-year percentage changes, and (4) the primary use cases tied to each language. I will retrieve the official 2024 and 2023 Octoverse language-distribution figures and corroborating sources, compute the required metrics, identify the largest growth language, and then run scenario-based projections for the next two years with rationale and cited sources.
